comments.author: @truehorizons66
comments.text: Brazil’s natural wonders are absolutely breathtaking. From the Amazon to waterfalls, wetlands, mountains, and wild coastlines, this country feels like an entire world of nature in one place. 🇧🇷

comments.author: @SergioluizDaCruz-r4c
comments.text: Como sempre, por mais que videos mostram o Brasil,  nenhum, mas nenhum mesmo, sequer arranha a diversidade do Brasil. Isso aí não dá 10% da realidade.

comments.author: @wakeuppeople777
comments.text: It would have been nice to see the miniature frogs, and the gold in that church you talked about. There were several other things you talked about but did not show. Disappointing.

comments.author: @Earthcue
comments.text: Brazil has over 4,000 airports — more than almost any country on Earth — because the Amazon is so massive there's literally no other way to get around! 😂

replies.author: @trueglobe66
replies.text: It’s truly incredible how the sheer scale of the Amazon shapes the way people connect and travel across the country! 🇧🇷✨

comments.author: @mbi43
comments.text: Great series, but...  Get rid of that AI narration.  The text is great, but AI narration is irritating.

replies.author: @trueglobe66
replies.text: I appreciate your honest feedback on the narration, and I will keep your thoughts in mind for future projects! ✨

comments.author: @ingriddurden3929
comments.text: I knew Brazil was incredible.  But how strange... a flooding town.  These clear lakes, the dunes with swimming pools in between, the sinkhole.   Did anyone else notice how sometimes 2 pics seem to sit over each other?  or is that meant to be?  Just added to the strangeness of the place, not interrupting.

comments.author: @trueunrealglobe
comments.text: Every new location introduced another reason to keep watching. I genuinely had no idea what incredible place would appear next.

replies.author: @trueglobe66
replies.text: It’s wonderful to hear that the diversity of Brazil’s landscapes kept you engaged throughout the documentary! ❤️
